title: Error: netavark: setns: IO error: Operation not permitted (os error 1) when running container using Podman
body:
### Issue Description

I tried to install and use Podman in running Docker container according to the installation instructions: https://podman.io/docs/installation however received an error described below.
I use Windows 10 with latest WSL 2.
I tried to issue this request in the container "curl -v https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/" and received **401 Unauthorized**.  However on my host machine **docker run hello-world** runs without errors.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create Dockerfile:
```
FROM ubuntu

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y podman
```

2. Build and run this container: **docker run -it ubuntu-podman**
3. Run hello-world container: podman run hello-world


### Describe the results you received

WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers
Resolved "hello-world" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/hello-world:latest...
Error: initializing source docker://hello-world:latest: pinging container registry registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": x509: certificate signed by unknown authority

### Describe the results you expected

hello-world container should be run without any issues.

### podman info output

comments.body:
I also tried to install Podman locally on my Windows 10 machine and noticed the difference.
When I ran **docker run hello-world** the log message stated:

```
Resolved "hello-world" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)                                                                                                                        
Trying to pull quay.io/podman/hello:latest...
```

So URI here is _quay.io/podman/hello:latest._ However when I executed the same command in Docker container the URI is different (_docker.io/library/hello-world:latest_)

I tried to use the latest Ubuntu version (24.04) and that gave me Postman 4.9.2 however when I ran **podman run hello-world** I got another error:

```
Resolved "hello-world" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/hello-world:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob c1ec31eb5944 done   |
Copying config d2c94e258d done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
ERRO[0003] Unmounting /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ee4992817b421b303a7b34273bfff27056a21974026c9be05e8245572acabe6a/merged: invalid argument
ERRO[0003] Preparing container e1f0906134cd189655f9ab63a705e4bcc77c0d363d7e5479665778c388b63d35: netavark: setns: IO error: Operation not permitted (os error 1)
Error: mounting storage for container e1f0906134cd189655f9ab63a705e4bcc77c0d363d7e5479665778c388b63d35: creating overlay mount to /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ee4992817b421b303a7b34273bfff27056a21974026c9be05e8245572acabe6a/merged, mount_data="lowerdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/LNV7EXKLYGV22OW4EKKJQBVNRA,upperdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ee4992817b421b303a7b34273bfff27056a21974026c9be05e8245572acabe6a/diff,workdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/ee4992817b421b303a7b34273bfff27056a21974026c9be05e8245572acabe6a/work": using mount program /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs: unknown argument ignored: lazytime
fuse: device not found, try 'modprobe fuse' first
fuse-overlayfs: cannot mount: No such file or directory
: exit status 1
```

I followed advice in one of the tickets here and added this instruction to Dockerfile: 

`VOLUME /var/lib/containers`

However another error appeared when running container:

`Error: netavark: setns: IO error: Operation not permitted (os error 1)`

I also tried Alpine Linux as base OS (Podman 4.8.3) however still got the same error:

`Error: netavark: setns: IO error: Operation not permitted (os error 1)`

Please keep one issue for one problem otherwise it is way to hard to follow.

It would help if you show how you actually run you other container? Is it privileged? If not it is more than likely that many things fail. 

I suggest you start with trying to run `quay.io/podman/stable` first as this contains many options to make it more compatible for in container use cases.
@Luap99 Ok, thank you for the suggestion. I will create new issue with updated description.
